---
title: "Boxplot hPu hPy repeat length"
output: 
date: "2026-02-20"
---

######################################################################################################
# Gustave Roussy (GR) | CNRS UMR 9019, Genome dynamics and instability Lab - Gabriel MATOS RODRIGUES 
# Developed by BEKKOUCHE Louisa - bekkouche.louisa@gmail.com                                 
# Edited by: Genome dynamics and instability Lab                                     
# v.0.1 - 2025/12/18 (for Human genome) | v.0.2 2026/02/16 (for Mouse genenome)	     
# R version 4.5.2 (2025-10-31)                                                       
# MIT licence - Copyright (c) 2025 Gabriel Matos Rodrigues			                     
# run it in a env conda for R version and enjoy your results! :)                     
#######################################################################################################

```{r}

#######################################################################################################

# Boxplots of repeat lengths from multiple BED groups
# - Reads several BED files (whole genome, hPu/hPy, neg/pos)
# - Computes repeat lengths
# - Draws custom quantile boxplots (P10–P90 whiskers)
# Dependencies: dplyr, ggplot2, readr (or base read.table), scales (optional)

#######################################################################################################

# Packages installation CRAN + BIOCONDUCTOR

cran_pkgs <- c("dplyr","ggplot2","scales")

# Utility function: installs only missing packages
install_missing <- function(pkgs) {
  missing <- pkgs[!pkgs %in% rownames(installed.packages())]
  if (length(missing) > 0) {
    install.packages(missing, repos = "https://cloud.r-project.org")
  }
}

# Install missing CRAN packages
install_missing(cran_pkgs)

message("All required packages are installed.")

library(dplyr)
library(ggplot2)
library(scales)

#######################################################################################################

# User inputs (adjust paths)
bed_whole_path  <- "put the whole genome bed file path"

bed_hPu_neg_path <- "put the hPu neg bed file path"
bed_hPy_neg_path <- "put the hPy neg bed file path"

bed_hPu_pos_path <- "put the hPu pos bed file path"
bed_hPy_pos_path <- "put the hPy pos bed file path"

out_plot <- "put the plot output path"

# Input checks
in_files <- c(bed_whole_path, bed_hPu_neg_path, bed_hPy_neg_path, bed_hPu_pos_path, bed_hPy_pos_path)
stopifnot("One or more input BED files do not exist." = all(file.exists(in_files)))

# Load BED files (expects: chrom, start, end, motif)
bed_whole  <- read.table(bed_whole_path,  sep = "\t", header = FALSE, stringsAsFactors = FALSE)
bed_hPu_neg <- read.table(bed_hPu_neg_path, sep = "\t", header = FALSE, stringsAsFactors = FALSE)
bed_hPy_neg <- read.table(bed_hPy_neg_path, sep = "\t", header = FALSE, stringsAsFactors = FALSE)
bed_hPu_pos <- read.table(bed_hPu_pos_path, sep = "\t", header = FALSE, stringsAsFactors = FALSE)
bed_hPy_pos <- read.table(bed_hPy_pos_path, sep = "\t", header = FALSE, stringsAsFactors = FALSE)

# Standardize columns and compute length
process_bed <- function(df, sample_name) {
  # Ensure at least 3 columns: chrom, start, end; 4th (motif) optional
  stopifnot("Each BED must have >= 3 columns (chrom, start, end)." = ncol(df) >= 3)
  colnames(df)[1:3] <- c("chrom", "start", "end")
  if (ncol(df) >= 4) {
    colnames(df)[4] <- "motif"
  } else {
    df$motif <- NA_character_
  }
  df$sample    <- sample_name
  df$length_bp <- df$end - df$start
  df
}

# Apply to all datasets
bed_whole   <- process_bed(bed_whole,   "Whole genome")
bed_hPu_neg <- process_bed(bed_hPu_neg, "hPu Neg")
bed_hPy_neg <- process_bed(bed_hPy_neg, "hPy Neg")
bed_hPu_pos <- process_bed(bed_hPu_pos, "hPu Pos")
bed_hPy_pos <- process_bed(bed_hPy_pos, "hPy Pos")

# Combine groups
bed_all <- rbind(
  bed_whole,
  bed_hPu_neg, bed_hPy_neg,
  bed_hPu_pos, bed_hPy_pos
)

# Order of samples on the x-axis
bed_all$sample <- factor(
  bed_all$sample,
  levels = c("Whole genome", "hPu Neg", "hPy Neg", "hPu Pos", "hPy Pos")
)

# Line colors for each group (used as box outline colors)
contour_colors <- c(
  "Whole genome" = "black",
  "hPu Neg"      = "firebrick",
  "hPy Neg"      = "springgreen4",
  "hPu Pos"      = "firebrick",
  "hPy Pos"      = "springgreen4"
)

# Custom summary function for stat_summary (P10–Q1–Median–Q3–P90)
q_box <- function(y) {
  data.frame(
    ymin   = quantile(y, 0.10, na.rm = TRUE),  # lower whisker = P10
    lower  = quantile(y, 0.25, na.rm = TRUE),  # Q1
    middle = quantile(y, 0.50, na.rm = TRUE),  # Median
    upper  = quantile(y, 0.75, na.rm = TRUE),  # Q3
    ymax   = quantile(y, 0.90, na.rm = TRUE)   # upper whisker = P90
  )
}

# Summary stats (used to annotate medians)
stats_df <- bed_all |>
  dplyr::group_by(sample) |>
  dplyr::summarise(
    median_len = median(length_bp, na.rm = TRUE),
    .groups = "drop"
  )

# Y-axis upper limit (fixed)
y_top <- 300

# Boxplot: quantile-based boxplots + median text labels
p_box <- ggplot(bed_all, aes(x = sample, y = lengh, color = sample)) +
  stat_summary(fun.data = q_box,geom = "boxplot",width = 0.7,size = 0.6,fill = "white") +
  geom_text(data = stats_df,aes(x = sample,y = median_len,label = paste0("Median = ", round(median_len, 1))),inherit.aes = FALSE,size = 3,color = "black",vjust = -0.6) +
  scale_color_manual(values = contour_colors) +
  labs(x = "", y = "Repeat length (bp)") +
  theme_classic(base_size = 14) +
  theme(legend.position = "none") +
  coord_cartesian(ylim = c(0, y_top))

print(p_box)


# Save figure
ggsave(
  filename = out_plot,
  plot     = p_box,
  width    = 7,
  height   = 6,
  units    = "in"
)

#######################################################################################################
```
